title: Our reality is already an illusion so how is HIP bad
display_title: Our reality is already an illusion so how is HIP bad
wikitext:
[]: Our reality is already an illusion so how is HIP bad?             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Fri Feb 11, 2011 4:00 pm I'll simplify this as much as possible. Our world and all in it are made of atoms>Atoms are mostly empty space>Atoms are composed of particles called protons, neutrons, and electrons. Protons and neutrons = made of different quarks Electrons = leptons String Theory: Quarks and Leptons are made of tiny strings (non physical) thus Matter = Energy = Your reality is illusion. If you think about it the soul, if it even exist, is the only true existing reality. If we look at death from a nonhuman state of mind, it is just a deactivation of illusionary flesh machines we humans define as bodies. Human instrumental project simply brings about true reality = Pure soul world because that's the only truth there is when asking what is real and what is not real. I believe this is what is meant by Seele's idea of human evolution. We are going beyond this false fake solid world that isn't even solid because solid is illusion.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Feb 11, 2011 4:04 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Feb 11, 2011 4:17 pm Well first of all, string theory (and all quantum mechanics, really) is not exactly well grounded or understood very well at the moment. It's only one of many competing ideas over how all that stuff works, and we have a long way to go for us to understand it better. So it's a bit of stretch to just assume that HIP would bring us closer to true reality. But even then, it's not as if true reality automatically makes things better. Humans are flawed creatures and we enjoy our flawed worlds. Shinji ultimately chose the flawed world with AT Fields because, as a human, he felt is was necessary to feel the positive emotions it would bring, even though it would bring negative ones as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 11, 2011 4:18 pm See also "Simulation Argument"

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 11, 2011 5:10 pm Because it's more than just uploading people to a false reality; it's taking away people's individuality and turning them into a single being. It's not "virtual reality" so much as "hive mind."

[]: Re: Our reality is already an illusion so how is HIP bad?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Feb 11, 2011 5:19 pm Ritsuko wrote: String Theory: Quarks and Leptons are made of tiny strings (non physical) thus Matter = Energy = Your reality is illusion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 11, 2011 5:28 pm Energy is still real in any case (unless nothing we typically think of as real actually is), and what needs to be done is not discounting reality but rather making the definition of reality more precise. That said, I think it's a grave mistake to discount the possibilities of such a perspective shift out of hand simply because it's different and the unknown is scary. Seele00TextOnly wrote: Well I'll go out on a limb here and suggest that what's 'bad' about HIP is that they're making that decision for everyone else on Earth against their will.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Feb 11, 2011 7:03 pm NemZ wrote: Free will doesn't exist.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 11, 2011 7:59 pm TehDonutKing wrote: So, what horrible sin put you in such grief as to subscribe to such denial as your rationalization?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Feb 11, 2011 9:08 pm + 1 to NemZ’s post.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Feb 11, 2011 9:57 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 11, 2011 11:06 pm NemZ wrote: "Theory" does not mean the same thing as "guess".

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 11, 2011 11:57 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Speak for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Feb 12, 2011 12:07 am So when I sit there for a solid minute strategising in a game of chess, I'm still only acting on impulse?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 12, 2011 12:10 am NemZ wrote: Seele

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Feb 12, 2011 1:41 am TehDonutKing wrote: So when I sit there for a solid minute strategising in a game of chess, I'm still only acting on impulse?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 12, 2011 1:54 am NemZ: All this talk about zillions of variables making any one decision at any given moment inevitable, and all I can think about is the law of probability. I have no idea how this could be scientifically tested, but according to my vestigial understanding of the law, even an identical situation, if enacted multiple times, should nonetheless result in variations. The idea of only a single outcome being possible seems fundamentally flawed to me. Your arguments against "free will" also use the very decisions that make a particular event "inevitable" as reason to think that only one outcome is possible. This leads straight into an infinite recursion, all the way back to the Big Bang, in which every single event is predetermined and inevitable. If so, I have no recourse but to put on my troll face until I see something a little more convincing than a philosophical argument. If I've misunderstood you in any way, apologies.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Feb 12, 2011 1:55 am NemZ wrote: Were you scared when you parents first taught you to swim or ride a bike, perhaps against your will? Were you still mad after realizing that they had your best interests in mind...

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Sat Feb 12, 2011 7:22 am Even without string theory it doesn't change the fact this constructed world around us is an illusion. Real or fake doesn't change the truth about death and life. As stated before, death is just a deactivation or change in this illusion unlocking you from (the illusionary worlds) the body. As for freewill and choice: We may think we're are adults but truly humanity as a whole is a baby that has to be forced into a new stage of growth. Mankind if continued down the path we are now, aren't really advancing beyond this grand illusion of false freewill. HIP: I don't believe it would have stopped with man becoming one. The state of being in one form I think is just a womb. Ultimately, in order to advance beyond even that stage we would have to separate but this time we are all newer more connected nonhuman beings able to create a deeper reality to learn from. In all honesty, man is at a dead end as long as we desire to be human.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Feb 12, 2011 8:52 am Troll or not - why is HIP bad? Because its not nice to get Tanged without giving permission first :P
